questions.original_question: 路特斯科技与吉利汽车的关系是什么？
questions.answer:
路特斯科技与吉利汽车之间的关系主要体现在以下几个方面：首先，吉利汽车和路特斯科技由同一实际控制人李书福最终控制。吉利汽车约75%的股份由李书福实际控制，而路特斯科技的单一最大股东Lotus Advanced Technology Limited Partnership也由李书福实际控制，持有路特斯科技27.6%的股份。其次，路特斯科技与吉利控股签署了多项协议，包括技术许可、制造合作和供应框架等，以确保供应链相关风险得到有效控制，并加速产品开发。这些合作使路特斯科技能够利用吉利控股的制造能力、采购和供应链的议价能力、资本投资和运营支持，从而加快其车辆的市场投放速度。此外，路特斯科技与吉利控股之间的战略合作伙伴关系始于2017年，当时吉利控股收购了路特斯英国51%的股份，并将路特斯科技定位为豪华生活方式电池电动车（BEV）制造商。双方签署了为期10年的制造协议，从2022年开始，吉利控股负责车辆的生产，而路特斯科技则主要负责车辆的设计与开发。这种合作模式帮助路特斯科技加速了产品上市，并确保了品牌的高质量标准。

以上信息基于2025年4月16日之前的知识。
rewritten_question: What is the relationship between Lotus Technology and Geely Automotive?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology views the manufacturers and suppliers it collaborates with as key partners throughout the vehicle development process. Lotus Technology aims to leverage its partners’ industry expertise to ensure that each vehicle produced meets the company's strict quality standards. Lotus Technology's Collaboration with Geely Holding Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with its major shareholder, Geely Holding, and its asset-light business model is supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of its vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for vehicle production and authorized Geely Holding to access its technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the relationship between Lotus Technology and Geely Automotive?

rag_info.chunk_content: Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between the traditional sports car and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable Lotus Technology to effectively raise its brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family. This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the relationship between Lotus Technology and Geely Automotive?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, such as agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource-related supporting services with our partners. strategic partner, Geely Holding. Lotus Technology's reliance on these agreements subjects the company to a number of significant risks, including the risk of being unable to operate as a standalone business, launch new vehicles, reach development and production targets, or focus efforts on core areas of differentiation. Of particular importance for Lotus Technology's operations are the related party agreements with Geely Holding and its affiliate entities. These related party agreements include the Technology License Agreement, the Manufacture Cooperation Agreement, and the Supply of Framework Agreement, amongst other areas. These agreements are described in more detail in this prospectus under “Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions — Related Party Transactions.” These partnerships permit Lotus Technology to benefit from Geely Holding’s decades of experience investing in established auto-manufacturers while focusing its efforts on core areas of differentiation, such as design, research and development, performance, and rapid adoption of the latest technologies and sustainability solutions. Lotus Technology intends to continue to rely on its partnership with Geely Holding as part of its strategy. Collaboration with Geely Holding for research and development, procurement, manufacturing, and engineering is subject to risks with respect to operations that are outside of Lotus Technology's control.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the relationship between Lotus Technology and Geely Automotive?

rag_info.chunk_content: NEW YORK, April 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced that Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely”) exercised its put option on April 14, 2025, requiring Lotus Technology Inc. to purchase 51% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (“Lotus UK”) held by Geely pursuant to the Put Option Agreement dated January 31, 2023 between the parties. Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd controls the manufacturing operations for Lotus Technology Inc.'s sportscars and hypercars, and Lotus Engineering provides comprehensive consultancy services to many of the OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers around the world. Pursuant to the exercise of the put option from Geely, Lotus Technology Inc. expects to acquire 51% of the equity interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd through a non-cash transaction based on a pre-agreed pricing method. Upon completion, Lotus Technology Inc. will gain control over Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and consolidate its financial results. The strategic transaction will enable Lotus Technology Inc. to integrate all businesses under the Lotus brand. The acquisition of 51% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd is expected to be completed by 2025, subject to potential regulatory approvals.
rag_info.sub_query: What is the relationship between Lotus Technology and Geely Automotive?

rag_info.chunk_content: Lotus Technology is a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider that designs and develops luxury lifestyle vehicles (non-sports car vehicles for daily usage) under the iconic British brand “Lotus” and sells all Lotus branded cars. With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes the market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. The Lotus brand was founded in the U.K. in 1948 and has since established itself as a high-performance luxury vehicle brand with innovative engineering and cutting-edge technologies, renowned for its superior aerodynamics and lightweight design. The Lotus brand was born out of legendary success on the racetrack, including 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles and many other championship honors. In 2017, Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in Lotus Technology and further set Lotus Technology up as a luxury lifestyle battery electric vehicle (BEV) maker. Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology views the manufacturers and suppliers that the company collaborates with as key partners throughout the vehicle development process. Lotus Technology aims to leverage its partners’ industry expertise to ensure that each vehicle produced meets the company's strict quality standards. Lotus Technology's Collaboration with Geely Holding Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with its major shareholder, Geely Holding, and its asset-light business model is supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of its vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for vehicle production and authorized Geely Holding to access its technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand. Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the intellectual properties necessary for vehicle production. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: The company recognizes its manufacturers and suppliers as vital partners in the vehicle development process, aiming to uphold high-quality standards through their industry expertise. Our strategic partnership with Geely Holding enhances this approach by combining our strengths in automotive innovation and technology. This collaboration focuses on developing advanced electric vehicle (EV) solutions, promoting sustainable mobility, and expanding our market presence. By leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities and operational support, we anticipate a faster market entry for our vehicles. 

Established in 2022, our 10-year manufacturing agreement with Geely allows them to produce our models while we retain responsibility for design, supplier selection, and maintaining Lotus brand standards. Geely will manage production logistics, quality control, and certification processes, all aligned with our quality assurance framework. Together, we are committed to driving the future of mobility, enhancing our competitive edge, and contributing to a greener transportation landscape.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: The "Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control" chapter highlights the critical role of manufacturers and suppliers in the vehicle development process, emphasizing the necessity of strict adherence to quality standards. A strategic partnership with Geely Holding, the major shareholder, underpins an asset-light business model that accelerates market entry by leveraging Geely's extensive manufacturing capacity, procurement power, capital investment, and operational support.

In 2022, a significant milestone was reached with the establishment of a 10-year manufacturing agreement with Geely Holding for vehicle production. This agreement delineates responsibilities, with the company retaining control over design, development, supplier designation, product announcements, and the maintenance of Lotus brand standards. Conversely, Geely Holding is responsible for sourcing raw materials, production planning, quality control, logistics, and plant operations, ensuring that quality assurance aligns with the company's established framework.

The manufacturing facility located in Wuhan, China, owned by Geely Holding, is specifically tailored for electric vehicles (EVs) and spans over one million square meters. This state-of-the-art plant is equipped with advanced manufacturing technologies, including stamping, welding, painting, assembly, and a dedicated quality inspection center. Additionally, the facility integrates intelligent driving technology for vehicle transport within workshops and features a 3 km track for quality testing, enabling high-speed evaluations of up to 230 km/h and providing various driving experiences for customers.

Overall, this chapter underscores the strategic collaboration with Geely Holding as a cornerstone of the manufacturing and supply chain strategy, ensuring that the company can efficiently produce high-quality vehicles while maintaining brand integrity and meeting market demands.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Manufacturing, Supply Chain, and Quality Control 
summary: Our collaboration with Geely Holding is a strategic partnership that plays a crucial role in our vehicle development process. By leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities and market presence alongside our technological advancements, we aim to drive innovation and accelerate the transition to sustainable mobility solutions, including electric vehicles. This partnership enhances our asset-light business model and facilitates the market introduction of our vehicles through Geely's procurement advantages and operational support. 

Under a 10-year manufacturing agreement initiated in 2022, Geely Holding has been authorized to access our technologies while we retain oversight of design, development, and brand standards. Geely is responsible for sourcing materials, production planning, quality control, and logistics, all while adhering to our quality assurance framework. This collaboration emphasizes research and development initiatives to create cutting-edge products that meet consumer demands and promote environmental sustainability, ultimately strengthening our competitive edge in the global automotive market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has established a strategic collaborative relationship with its major shareholder, Geely Holding, and its asset-light business model is supported by the Geely Holding ecosystem partners. Lotus Technology expects its relationship with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of its vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for vehicle production and authorized Geely Holding to access its technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand. Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the intellectual properties necessary for vehicle production. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with Lotus Technology's quality assurance framework and approved by Geely Holding. In addition, Geely Holding is responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Collaboration with Geely Holding 
summary: We have entered into a strategic partnership with our major shareholder, Geely Holding, aimed at enhancing our asset-light business model through the support of Geely's ecosystem partners. This collaboration is expected to accelerate the market launch of our vehicles by leveraging Geely Holding's manufacturing capabilities, procurement advantages, and operational support. As part of a 10-year manufacturing agreement initiated in 2022, Geely Holding is authorized to utilize our technologies while we retain control over design, development, supplier selection, product announcements, and upholding the global standards of the Lotus brand. We provide the necessary intellectual property for vehicle production, while Geely Holding manages the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, quality control, logistics, and overall manufacturing operations. Quality control will follow our assurance framework and requires our approval, with Geely Holding also responsible for the certification of the manufactured vehicles.

With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes the market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. Fusing proprietary next-generation technology built on world-class research and development capabilities and an asset-light model empowered by Geely Holding, Lotus Technology is breaking new ground in electrification, digitization, and intelligence. metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of luxury mobility solutions, focusing on the design and development of high-performance electric vehicles under the prestigious British brand "Lotus." With a rich heritage spanning over 70 years in motorsport, Lotus is renowned for its exceptional performance, innovative engineering, and lightweight design. Following Geely Holding's acquisition of a 51% stake in 2017, Lotus has evolved into a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, leveraging Geely's expertise in revitalizing automotive brands and its extensive support in manufacturing and R&D.

The luxury BEV market, characterized by vehicles priced over $80,000, is on the cusp of significant growth, and Lotus is at the forefront of this transformation. The launch of the Eletre, a fully electric Hyper-SUV, in 2022 marked our entry into this underserved segment, offering advanced performance features and rapid charging capabilities. The Eletre lineup includes three versions, with the high-performance Eletre R delivering impressive specifications, such as 905 horsepower and a 0 to 100 km/h acceleration in just 2.95 seconds. Additionally, the Emeya, our second lifestyle vehicle, is set to launch in 2024, with plans for more models in the pipeline.

Our competitive edge lies in our robust R&D capabilities, highlighted by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture (EPA), which enhances efficiency and flexibility across vehicle classes. This innovative platform, combined with our advanced driver assistance systems, positions us well in the market. We produce all BEV models in collaboration with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in China, which allows for scalability and cost-effectiveness.

To enhance customer engagement, we employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a comprehensive online platform for vehicle customization and aftersales services. With over 200 stores worldwide and a commitment to expanding our retail presence, we aim to deliver a luxury purchasing experience that reflects our brand's heritage and global aspirations.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: We are a leading global provider of luxury mobility solutions, focusing on the design and development of high-performance electric vehicles under the esteemed British brand "Lotus." With a rich history spanning over 70 years, Lotus is celebrated for its excellence in performance, engineering, and design, having secured 13 FIA Formula 1 world titles. Acquired by Geely Holding in 2017, Lotus is transitioning into a luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, leveraging Geely's expertise in manufacturing and supply chain management to innovate in electrification and intelligent mobility.

The luxury BEV market, particularly for vehicles priced over $80,000, is rapidly expanding yet remains underserved. Lotus is at the forefront of this sector, having launched its first fully electric Hyper-SUV, the Eletre, in 2022. This model features an advanced 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture, offering exceptional performance, with the Eletre R variant achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. Following the Eletre, Lotus introduced the Emeya in September 2023, with plans for additional models in the near future.

Central to Lotus's competitive edge is its robust R&D capabilities, supported by four facilities in Europe and China, which focus on advanced automotive technologies and a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). The collaboration with Geely allows Lotus to utilize advanced EV manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, enhancing scalability and minimizing capital investments.

Lotus also emphasizes a luxury retail experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, which includes premium stores and a global digital sales platform for personalized customer interactions. With over 200 stores worldwide and a commitment to expanding its retail network, Lotus is well-positioned to meet the growing demands of the luxury electric vehicle market while maintaining its heritage of high-performance sports cars.

With over seven decades of racing heritage and proven leadership in the automotive industry, the Lotus brand symbolizes market-leading standards in performance, design, and engineering. metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Business 
summary: We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer under the esteemed British brand "Lotus," which has over 70 years of racing heritage and is renowned for its performance, design, and engineering excellence. With the support of Geely Holding, which acquired a 51% stake in Lotus in 2017, we are transitioning from traditional sports cars to high-performance electric vehicles, leveraging cutting-edge technology and a robust research and development framework. 

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow significantly, is currently underserved, with only a handful of luxury BEV models available compared to a multitude of internal combustion engine options. As a leader in this sector, we aim to launch our E-segment BEV model and achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027. Our first fully electric model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, debuted in 2022, showcasing advanced technology and exceptional performance, with plans for further electric vehicle introductions in the coming years.

Our proprietary 800-volt Electric Power Architecture enables rapid model introductions and efficient production, while our Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS) incorporates cutting-edge features like deployable LiDAR. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at their advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, enhancing scalability and supply chain management.

We prioritize a luxury retail experience through a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating premium stores and a global digital platform that enables vehicle customization and direct customer engagement. With a growing global distribution network and plans to expand our store presence, we delivered 6,970 vehicles in 2023, including both lifestyle and sports cars, and are poised for continued growth in the luxury BEV market.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: Lotus is an innovative luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for creating high-end lifestyle vehicles that blend performance, design, and engineering excellence, backed by over 70 years of racing heritage. Founded in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has transitioned from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, especially after Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in 2017. This partnership has facilitated significant advancements in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and logistics, enabling Lotus to focus on electrification and intelligent automotive solutions.

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow at a CAGR of 35% from 2021 to 2031, is currently underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. Lotus aims to capitalize on this gap by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features an innovative 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture, offering exceptional performance, rapid charging capabilities, and a competitive price point. The company plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with additional models like the Emeya and a D-segment SUV set for future release.

Lotus emphasizes its R&D capabilities as a competitive advantage, leveraging its proprietary technologies and advanced driver assistance systems. The company operates five R&D facilities across the U.K., Germany, and China, fostering collaboration among skilled teams. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which allows for scalability and flexibility while minimizing capital investments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a global digital platform that includes a virtual showroom and customization options. The brand has established a global distribution network, with plans to expand its store count from 215 to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, reflecting its commitment to leading the electrification of luxury vehicles while maintaining strong ties to its British heritage.

Lotus Technology expects the partnership with Geely Holding to allow the company to bring its vehicles to the market at an accelerated pace by leveraging Geely Holding’s manufacturing capacity, bargaining power in procurement and supply chain, capital investment, and operational support. Lotus Technology entered into a manufacturing arrangement with Geely Holding for the manufacture of the Eletre and future models for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, Lotus Technology commissioned Geely Holding for the production of the Eletre and future models and authorized Geely Holding to access Lotus Technology's technologies for the production of such models. Lotus Technology is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand. Lotus Technology also provides Geely Holding with the necessary intellectual properties for the manufacture of the Eletre and future models. Geely Holding is mainly responsible for the ordering and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, logistics and transportation of manufactured vehicles, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with Lotus Technology's quality assurance framework and approved by Geely Holding. In addition, Geely Holding is responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles.

title: Our Collaboration with Geely Holding

This collaboration is expected to accelerate the market launch of our vehicles by leveraging Geely's manufacturing capabilities, procurement strengths, and operational support. As part of a 10-year manufacturing agreement initiated in 2022, we will oversee the design, development, and branding of the Eletre and future models, while providing Geely with the necessary intellectual property. Geely will handle sourcing raw materials, production planning, quality control, logistics, and plant management, ensuring that all manufacturing processes meet our global quality standards and obtaining required vehicle certifications.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology has entered into a variety of agreements, such as agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource-related supporting services with our partners. strategic partner, Geely Holding. Lotus Technology's reliance on these agreements subjects the company to a number of significant risks, including the risk of being unable to operate as a standalone business, launch new vehicles, reach development and production targets, or focus efforts on core areas of differentiation. Of particular importance for Lotus Technology's operations are the related party agreements with Geely Holding and its affiliate entities. These related party agreements include the Technology License Agreement, the Manufacture Cooperation Agreement, and the Supply of Framework Agreement, amongst other areas. These agreements are described in more detail in this prospectus under “Certain Relationships and Related Person Transactions — Related Party Transactions.” These partnerships permit Lotus Technology to benefit from Geely Holding’s decades of experience investing in established auto-manufacturers while focusing its efforts on core areas of differentiation, such as design, research and development, performance, and rapid adoption of the latest technologies and sustainability solutions. Lotus Technology intends to continue to rely on its partnership with Geely Holding as part of its strategy. Collaboration with Geely Holding for research and development, procurement, manufacturing, and engineering is subject to risks with respect to operations that are outside of Lotus Technology's control.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our reliance on a variety of arrangements with Geely Holding, such as agreements related to research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, and IT and human resource related supporting service, could subject us to risks. 
summary: The organization has formed various agreements with Geely Holding, covering areas such as research and development, procurement, manufacturing, engineering, IT, and human resources. This partnership is crucial for the company's operations, enabling it to leverage Geely's extensive automotive industry experience while focusing on its strengths in design, technology integration, and sustainability. However, the reliance on Geely also introduces significant operational risks, including the potential inability to operate independently, launch new vehicle models, and meet production goals. The company faces challenges such as the risk of delays, cost overruns, and quality assurance issues, which could adversely affect its financial condition and growth prospects. Disputes with Geely or negative perceptions of its vehicle quality could further impact the company's brand. Moreover, the inability to maintain this collaboration may hinder the establishment of new agreements with third-party manufacturers, complicating operations and financial performance. The partnership also influences supply chain efficiency, as Geely's bargaining power aids in securing favorable terms with suppliers. A breakdown in this relationship could lead to difficulties in supplier negotiations and increased costs due to price fluctuations. Overall, the organization’s reliance on Geely Holding is a double-edged sword, providing essential support while posing significant risks to its operational and financial stability.

title: EXHIBIT INDEX 
summary: On June 24, 2024, Lotus Technology Inc. finalized a Convertible Note Purchase Agreement with Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited, allowing Geely to invest in Lotus through senior convertible notes. This arrangement grants Geely the option to convert the debt into equity in the future, creating opportunities for mutual growth. In a press release, Lotus Technology emphasized the importance of this investment as a strategic partnership that is poised to bolster its capabilities and enhance its market position in the industry.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: The Group entered into a manufacturing agreement with Geely Auto Wuhan Branch and Wuhan Geely Auto Parts (collectively as “OEMs”) for the manufacture of the Group’s electrical vehicles for 10 years starting from 2022. Pursuant to the manufacturing agreement, the Group commissioned the OEMs for the production of electrical vehicles and agreed to authorize the OEMs to access the Group’s technologies for the production of such models. The Group is mainly responsible for the design and development of the models, designation of suppliers, product announcement, and ensuring consistency with global standards of the Lotus brand. The Group also provides the OEMs with the necessary intellectual properties for the manufacture of electrical vehicles. The OEMs are mainly responsible for the procurement and inspection of raw materials, production planning, production quality control, and construction and operation of the manufacturing plant. Particularly, quality control is carried out in accordance with the Group’s quality assurance framework and is approved by the OEMs. In addition, the OEMs are responsible for obtaining certificates for the manufactured vehicles.

metadata.title_summary:
title: (d) Manufacturing agreement with the Geely Group 
summary: The Group has established a 10-year manufacturing partnership with Geely Auto Wuhan Branch and Wuhan Geely Auto Parts, commencing in 2022, to produce electric vehicles. In this collaboration, the Group will lead the design and development process, manage supplier selections, announce new products, and uphold global Lotus brand standards, while also providing essential intellectual property to the OEMs. Conversely, the OEMs will be responsible for sourcing and inspecting raw materials, planning production, maintaining quality control, and operating the manufacturing facility to ensure compliance with the Group's quality framework. They will also be responsible for securing the necessary certifications for the vehicles produced.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: NEW YORK, April 16, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Lotus Technology Inc. (“Lotus Tech” or the “Company”) (Nasdaq: LOT), a leading global intelligent and luxury mobility provider, today announced that Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited (“Geely”) exercised its put option on April 14, 2025, requiring Lotus Technology Inc. to purchase 51% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (“Lotus UK”) held by Geely pursuant to the Put Option Agreement dated January 31, 2023 between the parties. Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd controls the manufacturing operations for Lotus Technology Inc.'s sportscars and hypercars, and Lotus Engineering provides comprehensive consultancy services to many of the OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers around the world. Pursuant to the exercise of the put option from Geely, Lotus Technology Inc. expects to acquire 51% of the equity interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd through a non-cash transaction based on a pre-agreed pricing method. Upon completion, Lotus Technology Inc. will gain control over Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and consolidate its financial results. The strategic transaction will enable Lotus Technology Inc. to integrate all businesses under the Lotus brand. The acquisition of 51% of the equity interests in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd is expected to be completed by 2025, subject to potential regulatory approvals.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Lotus Technology to Acquire $5 1 \%$ Equity Interest of Lotus Advance Technologies and Integrate All Business under Lotus Brand 
summary: On April 16, 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. announced that Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited has exercised its put option, requiring Lotus Tech to acquire a 51% stake in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (Lotus UK). This acquisition aligns with a Put Option Agreement established on January 31, 2023, and is expected to be finalized by 2025, pending regulatory approvals. Lotus UK is responsible for producing Lotus' sportscars and hypercars, as well as managing Lotus Engineering, which provides consultancy services to OEMs and Tier 1 suppliers worldwide.

The acquisition will enable Lotus Tech to consolidate its financial results and enhance the integration of its businesses under the Lotus brand. The agreement stipulates that the acquisition is contingent upon Lotus UK and its subsidiaries selling over 5,000 vehicles in 2024, a condition that has already been met as of December 31, 2024. CEO Mr. Qingfeng Feng emphasized that this strategic move aims to strengthen brand equity, improve operational flexibility, and create internal synergies, ultimately generating long-term value for shareholders. In exchange for the Lotus UK shares, the Company plans to issue new shares priced at $10 each, with Lotus UK's valuation set at 1.15 times its revenue, adjusted for cash and debt, based on the 2024 audited financial report.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: In conjunction with the business combination between Lotus Technology Inc. and L Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, a special purpose acquisition company affiliated with L Catterton, Lotus Technology Inc. and Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd entered into a Put Option Agreement with each of Geely and Etika. Pursuant to these agreements, each of Geely and Etika was granted the right to require Lotus Technology Inc. to acquire its equity interest in Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd at a pre-agreed pricing method and upon satisfaction of the condition (“Put Option Exercise Condition”) that the total number of vehicles sold by Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd and its subsidiaries shall exceed 5,000 in 2024, with the exercise of such options by Geely and Etika not cross-conditioned on one another. As of December 31, 2024, the Put Option Exercise Condition had been satisfied. Mr. Qingfeng Feng, Chief Executive Officer, said: "This acquisition marks a critical milestone in Lotus Technology Inc.'s strategic journey to fully integrate all businesses under the Lotus brand, which will strengthen brand equity and enhance operational flexibility and internal synergies. Lotus Technology Inc. is confident that the transaction will create substantial long-term value for its shareholders.” Note 1: Pursuant to the agreement, Lotus Technology Inc. will issue new shares valued at $10 per share in consideration of Geely and Etika’s transfer of Lotus UK shares. Lotus UK is valued at 1.15 times revenue plus cash minus debt of Lotus Global Investment Limited (LGIL), based on LGIL’s audited consolidated annual financial report of 2024.

title: otus Technology to Acquire $1 0 0 \%$ Equity Interest
of Lotus Advance Technologies and Integrate All Business and Operations under Lotus Brand 
summary: On July 1, 2025, Lotus Technology Inc. (Nasdaq: LOT) announced that Etika Automotive Sdn Bhd exercised its put option on June 30, 2025, compelling Lotus Tech to acquire 49% of Lotus Advance Technologies Sdn Bhd (Lotus UK). This follows Geely International (Hong Kong) Limited's earlier exercise of a put option on April 14, 2025. As a result, Lotus Tech is set to gain full control of Lotus UK through non-cash transactions at pre-agreed prices, allowing for the consolidation of its financial results and integration of operations under the Lotus brand. Lotus UK is responsible for producing Lotus sportscars and hypercars and offers consultancy services through Lotus Engineering. The acquisition is expected to be completed by 2025, subject to regulatory approvals. The agreements with Geely HK and Etika were established as part of Lotus Tech's business combination with $L$ Catterton Asia Acquisition Corp, with the condition that Lotus UK and its subsidiaries sell over 5,000 vehicles in 2024, a threshold that has already been met as of December 31, 2024. In exchange for the shares of Lotus UK, Lotus Tech will issue new shares priced at $10 each, with the company's valuation based on a multiple of 1.15 times its revenue, adjusted for cash and debt, according to its 2024 audited financial report.

metadata.title_summary:
title: LOTUS TECHNOLOGY INC. NOTES TO THE CONSOLIDATED AND COMBINED FINANCIAL STATEMENTS (All amounts in thousands, except for share and per share data) 
 
summary: ### Summary of Business and Organizational Overview

Lotus Technology Inc. operates in the automotive and technology sectors, focusing on electric and autonomous vehicles. The company emphasizes innovation, sustainability, and advanced engineering, aiming to enhance user experience through smart connectivity and intelligent systems. Its organizational structure includes a significant number of ordinary shares, with a notable portion held by the LCA Acquisition Sponsor LP and public shareholders. The company has undergone various mergers and acquisitions, including a reorganization to streamline operations and enhance control over its subsidiaries.

As of February 23, 2024, Lotus Technology's ordinary shares and Public Warrants began trading on the Nasdaq under the symbols "LOT" and "LOTWW." The company has issued multiple types of financial instruments, including Public Warrants, Sponsor Warrants, and exchangeable notes, which have been categorized based on their fair value measurements. The financial statements reflect a continuation of operations post-merger, treating the mergers as recapitalizations without recognizing goodwill or intangible assets.

The company has established Variable Interest Entity (VIE) arrangements to maintain control over its subsidiaries in compliance with Chinese regulations. These arrangements allow Lotus Technology to consolidate the financial results of its VIEs, despite not having direct ownership. However, uncertainties in the Chinese legal system pose risks to the enforceability of these arrangements, potentially impacting the company's operations and financial condition.

Lotus Technology has also engaged in significant related party transactions, including agreements with Geely Holding and other subsidiaries, reflecting its interconnected business operations. The company has received various subsidies for research and development, alongside government grants aimed at offsetting construction costs for its facilities.

Financially, the company has faced challenges, including increasing net losses and a deteriorating equity position, as reflected in its condensed balance sheet. The parent company's comprehensive loss has risen over the years, driven by operational expenses and losses from subsidiaries. Despite these challenges, Lotus Technology continues to explore funding strategies and operational efficiencies to enhance its market position.

In summary, Lotus Technology Inc. is positioned as a forward-thinking player in the automotive industry, navigating complex financial and regulatory landscapes while striving for innovation and sustainability in its product offerings.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Geely Holding, a global mobility technology group with a proven track record in seeding BEV brands, has successfully incubated and revitalized a series of world-renowned brands with attractive financial profiles, including Volvo, Polestar, LYNK&CO., and Zeekr. Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between traditional sports cars and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a SPAC affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable the company to effectively raise brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: **Comprehensive Summary of the Overview Chapter**

We are a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the iconic British brand "Lotus," which boasts over 70 years of racing heritage. Since our acquisition by Geely Holding in 2017, we have been transitioning from a traditional sports car company to a luxury lifestyle BEV maker, leveraging Geely's extensive support in manufacturing, supply chain, and research and development (R&D).

The global luxury BEV market, defined as vehicles with a Manufacturer's Suggested Retail Price (MSRP) exceeding $80,000, is projected to experience significant growth, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 35% from 2021 to 2031, ultimately reaching nearly 1.9 million units by 2031. Currently, this market is underserved, featuring only about 10 luxury BEV models compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) luxury models.

Our first fully electric model, the Eletre, launched in 2022, is a luxury E-segment SUV powered by our proprietary 800-volt Electric Performance Architecture (EPA). The Eletre is available in three versions, with the Eletre R delivering an impressive 905 horsepower and achieving 0 to 100 km/h in just 2.95 seconds. We aim to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, having launched our second lifestyle vehicle, the Emeya, in September 2023, with deliveries scheduled to commence in March 2024. Future product launches include a D-segment SUV in 2025 and a BEV lifestyle sports car in 2026.

Our R&D capabilities serve as a competitive strength, supported by five facilities located in the U.K., Germany, and China. These facilities focus on developing advanced technologies, including a leading Advanced Driver Assistance System (ADAS). We manufacture our BEV models in partnership with Geely, utilizing their state-of-the-art facilities in Wuhan, China, with a planned annual production capacity of 150,000 units.

We employ a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, operating 215 stores globally as of December 31, 2023, with plans to expand to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, we successfully delivered 6,970 vehicles, encompassing both lifestyle and sports car models. Our collaboration with L Catterton, which has connections to LVMH, is anticipated to enhance our brand awareness and consumer insights, although no formal agreements with LVMH have been finalized as of this prospectus.

This strategic positioning within the luxury BEV market, combined with our innovative product offerings and robust R&D capabilities, positions us well for future growth and success in the evolving automotive landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: By maintaining a strong focus on research and development and innovation, Lotus Group expects to enjoy significant competitive advantages over other automotive brands in vehicle performance and driving experience. Asset-light business model supported by Geely Holding ecosystem. Lotus Group adopts an asset-light business model that leverages Geely Holding’s extensive resources in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development, logistics infrastructure, and human capital. Lotus Group benefits from Geely Holding’s newly constructed, state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities dedicated to electric vehicles in Wuhan, China, to manufacture its battery electric vehicle models, which allows the company to operate under a highly scalable model that can efficiently execute its business plan with limited upfront capital commitment. Leveraging Geely Holding’s global supply chain, Lotus Group can quickly establish and maintain relationships with top-tier global suppliers to secure stable supplies of critical components, particularly components such as batteries and automotive chips, which are susceptible to price volatility and supply disruption. Lotus Group believes the strong support from Geely Holding significantly enhances its ability to expand global operations more quickly, efficiently, and cost-effectively than other original equipment manufacturers. Focus on sustainability.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Strengths 
summary: The company is strategically positioned in the luxury Battery Electric Vehicle (BEV) market, leveraging its status as an early mover to capitalize on the rapid growth of this segment. It offers a diverse portfolio of BEV models priced between $80,000 and $149,000, which represents the largest volume contributor to the luxury BEV market, currently characterized as underserved and ripe for expansion.

With over 70 years of heritage in sports car design and engineering, the Lotus brand is renowned for its iconic status and racing pedigree, having secured multiple Formula 1 championships. The brand's excellence has been recognized with accolades such as "Luxury Brand of the Year" at the 2019 Luxury Briefing Awards and the 2020 MUSE Global Design Award for its Evija model.

The company is at the forefront of innovation, boasting proprietary next-generation technology that includes an 800-volt EPA architecture for supercharging and energy conservation, as well as advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) featuring the world’s first deployable LiDAR system. Its operating system, Lotus Hyper OS, enhances the passenger experience through real-time 3D content.

Adopting an asset-light business model, the company benefits significantly from its partnership with Geely Holding, which provides access to state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities in Wuhan, China. This collaboration facilitates scalable operations with minimal upfront capital and enables the establishment of relationships with top-tier suppliers, ensuring stable access to critical components.

Sustainability is a core focus, with the company aiming for carbon neutrality across its value chain by 2038. It has achieved an ESG rating of A- from SynTao Green Finance, placing it in the top 7% of listed companies evaluated. Additionally, its photovoltaic power generation system produced over 9,800 MWh in 2023.

The company employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, offering a seamless customer experience through the Lotus App and direct sales initiatives, while also partnering with automotive dealers for rapid market expansion.

Finally, the management team, led by industry veteran Mr. Qingfeng Feng, consists of experienced professionals from leading automotive firms, ensuring a strong commitment to innovation and design, which is critical for maintaining competitive advantages in the evolving luxury BEV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between the traditional sports car and a new generation of electric vehicles. Lotus Technology expects the business combination with LCAA, a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable Lotus Technology to effectively raise its brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family. This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Overview 
summary: Lotus is an innovative luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer, renowned for creating high-end lifestyle vehicles that blend performance, design, and engineering excellence, backed by over 70 years of racing heritage. Founded in the U.K. in 1948, Lotus has transitioned from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, especially after Geely Holding acquired a 51% stake in 2017. This partnership has facilitated significant advancements in manufacturing, research and development (R&D), and logistics, enabling Lotus to focus on electrification and intelligent automotive solutions.

The global luxury BEV market, projected to grow at a CAGR of 35% from 2021 to 2031, is currently underserved, with only about 10 luxury BEV models available compared to over 100 internal combustion engine (ICE) models. Lotus aims to capitalize on this gap by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features an innovative 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture, offering exceptional performance, rapid charging capabilities, and a competitive price point. The company plans to achieve a 100% BEV product portfolio by 2027, with additional models like the Emeya and a D-segment SUV set for future release.

Lotus emphasizes its R&D capabilities as a competitive advantage, leveraging its proprietary technologies and advanced driver assistance systems. The company operates five R&D facilities across the U.K., Germany, and China, fostering collaboration among skilled teams. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely at advanced facilities in Wuhan, China, which allows for scalability and flexibility while minimizing capital investments.

To enhance customer experience, Lotus employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales model, featuring premium stores and a global digital platform that includes a virtual showroom and customization options. The brand has established a global distribution network, with plans to expand its store count from 215 to over 300 by 2025. In 2023, Lotus delivered 6,970 vehicles, reflecting its commitment to leading the electrification of luxury vehicles while maintaining strong ties to its British heritage.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Positioned as the only Geely Holding-affiliated brand with sports car DNA, Lotus Technology has received comprehensive support from Geely Holding in manufacturing, supply chain, research and development (R&D), logistics infrastructure, and human capital, and is in the process of transforming from a British sports car company to a global pioneer of high-performance electric vehicles to bridge the gap between the traditional sports car and a new generation of electric vehicles. The proposed business combination with LCAA, a special purpose acquisition company (SPAC) affiliated with L Catterton, which has a strategic relationship with LVMH, is expected to provide significant support in consumer insights and brand collaboration that will enable Lotus Technology to effectively raise its brand awareness globally. L Catterton has an exclusive relationship for consumer-focused private equity with LVMH and Financière Agache, the main investment company of Bernard Arnault, LVMH’s chairman and chief executive officer, and his family. This relationship provides L Catterton with direct access to LVMH’s most senior executives, global consumer insights, industry network and contacts, differentiated investment deal flow, and collaboration on operating best practices. L Catterton retains sole discretion over all investment and portfolio management decisions related to the L Catterton funds. As of the date of this proxy statement/prospectus, Lotus Technology has not entered into any agreements with LVMH.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our business 
summary: The company is a pioneering luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) manufacturer operating under the prestigious British brand "Lotus," which has a rich history of over 70 years in racing and automotive excellence. Acquired by Geely Holding in 2017, Lotus is transitioning from a traditional sports car manufacturer to a leader in high-performance electric vehicles, supported by Geely's asset-light model and extensive R&D capabilities. The luxury BEV market is projected to grow significantly, and Lotus aims to capitalize on this opportunity by launching its E-segment BEV model, the Hyper-SUV Eletre, which debuted in 2022. The Eletre features advanced technology, impressive performance metrics, and a competitive price point, contributing to a strong global order book of over 8,600 units as of September 30, 2023.

In addition to the Eletre, the company plans to introduce the Emeya lifestyle vehicle and two more fully electric models by 2025. Its proprietary 800-volt Electric Powertrain Architecture enhances vehicle performance and production efficiency, while a cutting-edge Driver Assistance System incorporates advanced safety features. Manufacturing is conducted in partnership with Geely in China, leveraging their facilities and supply chain to ensure scalability and quality.

The company employs a digital-first, omni-channel sales strategy, enhancing customer engagement through personalized services and a global distribution network. With 206 stores worldwide and plans to expand to over 300 by 2025, Lotus is well-positioned to enhance its brand presence and meet the growing demand for luxury BEVs, supported by a cumulative order book exceeding 19,000 units as of late September 2023.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in Lotus Technology's competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, its focus on design and experience, its advanced technologies, its relationship with Geely Holding and therefore the benefits it can obtain during the vehicle development phase, and its future-proof approach give it a competitive edge and allow it to formulate a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that it believes generates significant competitive advantages, allowing it to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Competition 
summary: The company is navigating a competitive landscape marked by both established luxury automotive manufacturers and a rising number of emerging firms specializing in electric and alternative fuel vehicles. Key factors influencing the success of electric vehicle (EV) brands include brand recognition, design aesthetics, technological innovation, and overall driver experience. High standards of product quality, reliability, and safety are essential, as are advancements in battery technology, customer service, and competitive pricing strategies.

Despite its strengths, the company faces significant challenges from competitors with superior financial, technical, and operational resources. These rivals can invest heavily in product design, development, and marketing, benefiting from stronger brand recognition and extensive industry connections. The competitive environment is further complicated by ongoing mergers and acquisitions, which may consolidate resources among competitors.

To maintain its competitive edge, the company leverages its brand heritage, design focus, and advanced technologies, supported by a partnership with Geely Holding that enhances vehicle development. This collaboration allows for a distinct go-to-market strategy while operating under a scalable, asset-light business model that reduces upfront capital expenditures. This approach enables the company to invest more in research and development, fostering innovation and positioning itself for long-term success in the evolving EV market.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Additionally, many of these competitors also have greater name recognition, longer operating histories, larger sales forces, broader customer and industry relationships, and other tangible and intangible resources that exceed those of Lotus Technology. These competitors also compete with Lotus Technology in recruiting and retaining qualified research and development, sales, marketing, and management personnel, as well as in acquiring technologies complementary to, or necessary for, the company's products. Additional mergers and acquisitions in the electric vehicle and luxury automotive markets may result in even more resources being concentrated in Lotus Technology's competitors. Lotus Technology believes that its brand and history, focus on design and experience, advanced technologies, relationship with Geely Holding, and the benefits obtained during the vehicle development phase, along with its future-proof approach, provide a competitive edge and enable the formulation of a highly differentiated go-to-market strategy. Lotus Technology also has a scalable asset-light business model that is believed to generate significant competitive advantages, allowing the company to incur less upfront capital expenditure and focus on research and development and technologies.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Cybersecurity and Privacy 
summary: The organization places a strong emphasis on cybersecurity, privacy, and product security to foster trust among customers and employees. It employs a comprehensive array of technical and organizational security measures, backed by a dedicated team focused on application, network, system, and product security. The company has achieved notable certifications, including ISO/IEC 27001 and R155/R156, and has initiated corporate-wide data privacy policies that utilize cross-functional resources.

To manage vulnerabilities effectively, the organization has established enterprise vulnerability management processes that include periodic scans, internal and external penetration tests, and the use of threat intelligence to identify and remediate vulnerabilities based on severity and risk. A centralized view of information assets has been developed, complemented by cybersecurity risk monitoring policies designed to detect potential threats. Additionally, the organization has implemented cloud security policies that incorporate encryption and authentication measures to safeguard products, software, vehicles, and over-the-air (OTA) updates, alongside robust update procedures.

In vehicle development, cybersecurity is integrated through a Threat Analysis and Risk Assessment (TARA) process, which enhances resilience against potential attacks. The organization’s privacy policies strive to balance beneficial data use with customer privacy expectations, adhering to global privacy laws. It adopts privacy-by-design principles in both software and hardware development, emphasizing collaboration between IT and legal functions to strengthen privacy measures.

To promote cybersecurity and privacy awareness, the organization invests in education and training initiatives. The competitive landscape includes traditional luxury automotive developers and new entrants in the electric and alternative fuel vehicle sectors. Key competitive factors include brand recognition, technological innovation, product quality, customer service, and environmental impact. While the organization believes it is well-positioned to compete, it faces challenges from competitors with greater resources and market presence.

Leveraging its brand heritage, focus on design and experience, advanced technologies, and a strategic partnership with Geely Holding, the company has developed a differentiated market strategy. Its asset-light business model is designed to minimize upfront capital expenditure while emphasizing research and development (R&D) and technology advancement. This comprehensive approach ensures that the organization remains competitive and resilient in a rapidly evolving market landscape.

all_retrieved_content.page_content: Lotus Technology's exceptional technologies are demonstrated by its proprietary 800-volt EPA architecture entailing supercharging capabilities, high energy conservation, and high-speed data transmission, with high adaptability that can accommodate varying battery sizes, motors, and component layouts across vehicle classes. Additionally, Lotus Technology possesses leading ADAS technology with fully embedded L4-ready hardware capability, enabled by the world’s first deployable LiDAR system, five 360° perception coverage, and a self-developed software system for cognition, decision making, design, and control algorithms. Lotus Technology's operating system, Lotus Hyper OS, utilizes technology to create real-time 3D content for passengers. By maintaining a strong focus on R&D and innovation, Lotus Technology expects to enjoy significant competitive advantages over other automotive brands in vehicle performance and driving experience. Asset-light business model supported by Geely Holding ecosystem. Lotus Technology adopts an asset-light business model that leverages Geely Holding’s extensive resources in manufacturing, supply chain, R&D, logistics infrastructure, and human capital. Lotus Technology benefits from Geely Holding’s newly-constructed, state-of-the-art manufacturing facilities dedicated for EVs in Wuhan, China, with a production capacity of 150,000 units annually, to manufacture its BEV models, which allows Lotus Technology to operate under a highly scalable model that can efficiently execute its business plan with limited upfront capital commitment. Leveraging Geely Holding’s global supply chain, Lotus Technology can quickly establish and maintain relationships with top-tier global suppliers to secure stable supplies of critical components, particularly components such as batteries and automotive chips, which are susceptible to price volatility and supply disruption.

metadata.title_summary:
title: Our Strengths 
summary: The company is strategically positioned as a pioneer in the luxury battery electric vehicle (BEV) market, benefiting from its iconic brand heritage and advanced technological capabilities. With over 70 years of experience in sports car design and engineering, the brand is recognized for its commitment to aerodynamics and lightweight design, complemented by a successful motorsport legacy that includes multiple Formula 1 championships. 

Leveraging proprietary next-generation technologies developed through robust research and development, the company aims to offer a fully electric product portfolio, targeting the underserved luxury BEV segment with models priced between $80,000 and $149,000. The company plans to transition to a 100% BEV lineup by 2027, establishing a competitive edge in electrification.

Supported by Geely Holding's extensive resources, the company operates an asset-light business model that enhances operational efficiency and enables rapid global expansion. This partnership provides access to advanced manufacturing facilities and a reliable supply chain, ensuring stability in sourcing critical components.

The company is also committed to sustainability, adhering to high international ESG standards and aiming for a complete lineup of electric vehicles. Its digital-first, omni-channel sales model enhances the luxury retail experience, integrating online and physical services to meet customer needs effectively.

Guided by a visionary leadership team with extensive experience in the automotive industry, the company is well-positioned to maintain its competitive advantages and drive innovation in the luxury electric vehicle market.
